creator: eduardo@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hello,

I've done some tests with:
---
===> The following configuration options are available for root-6.28.04_1:
     DOCS=off: Build and/or install documentation
     MYSQL=off: Enable MySQL bindings
     PGSQL=off: Enable PostgreSQL bindings
     PYROOT=off: Enable bindings between Python and C++ with PyROOT
     ROOT7=off: Enable supplementary preview features of ROOT version 7
     SQLITE=off: Enable SQLite bindings
     X11=off: Enable interfacing with X11 graphics system
---

And noticed that configure shows that above options are not disabled:

-- PyROOT will be built for version 3.9.16
(pyroot is set to bolean and it isn't disabled)

-- Looking for MySQL
-- Could NOT find MySQL (missing: MYSQL_INCLUDE_DIR MYSQL_LIBRARIES)
-- MySQL not found. Switching off mysql option
(cmake turned mysql off because missing mysql, the correct will be turn off this check to avoid enabling it on system that have it installed)

-- Looking for PostgreSQL
-- Could NOT find PostgreSQL (missing: PostgreSQL_LIBRARY PostgreSQL_INCLUDE_DIR)
-- PostgreSQL not found. Switching off pgsql option
(same as above)

-- Looking for SQLite
-- Could NOT find PkgConfig (missing: PKG_CONFIG_EXECUTABLE)
-- Could NOT find Sqlite (missing: SQLITE_INCLUDE_DIR SQLITE_LIBRARIES)
-- SQLite not found. Switching off sqlite option
(same as above)

(...)

According to ${WRKSRC}/cmake/modules/RootBuildOptions.cmake, all build options are set to ON/OFF (not bolean)

Could you configure options with OPT_CMAKE_ON to check if build options are respected? 

Cheers
